title: Cris Pieterson
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:CrisPieterson.jpg]] |name=Cris Pieterson |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', which include Cris Pieterson's [[Death/Legends|death]] aboard the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Heir to the Empire 3|''Heir to the Empire'' 3]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Thrawn's confederation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Do you know the difference between an error and a mistake, Ensign?''"
"''No, sir.''"
"''Anyone can make an error, Ensign. But that error doesn't become a mistake until you refuse to correct it.|Grand Admiral Thrawn and Ensign Colclazure|}} '''Cris Pieterson''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was conscripted into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] service, being trained by [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Colclazure]]. During the [[Thrawn campaign]] of [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Pieterson served Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] aboard the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]. During the campaign, Thrawn attempted to capture [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] with one of the ''Chimaera''{{'s}} [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]]. Skywalker was able to destroy Pieterson's [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|tractor beam projector]], something Pieterson claimed he had not been trained to fight against, and fled. As a result, Thrawn spoke with Pieterson, who attempted to shift the blame onto Colclazure. Unhappy with the tractor beam operator's inability to adapt to new situations and his attempt to place the blame on his [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], Thrawn ordered his [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]] to cut off Pieterson's head, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him. ==Biography== {{Quote|I was never properly trained for such an occurrence, sir. ''[…]'' The computer lost the lock, but seemed to pick it up again right away. There was no way for me to know it had really picked up something else until—|Cris Pieterson, to Grand Admiral Thrawn|}} [[File:CrisPietersonDeath.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Rukh beheads Pieterson]] Cris Pieterson was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was conscripted into serving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], eventually being trained by [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Colclazure]] to serve as a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] operator aboard the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and his [[Thrawn's confederation|confederation]]. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], during the [[Thrawn campaign]], the ''Chimaera'' attempted to capture [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], bringing his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] with an [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor Cruiser]]. Once the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was out of hyperspace, Pieterson locked one of the ''Chimaera''{{'s}} tractor beams on the X-wing; however, Skywalker fired several [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] at the ''Chimaera'', which were captured by the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] tractor beam, pulling them in to one tractor beam projector. After the projector was destroyed, Skywalker lanced his starfighter upward and fled to hyperspace. Thrawn was displeased with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] escape, and walked to Pieterson's station. The Grand Admiral asked the other if Skywalker's escape had been his fault, prompting the conscript to claim that his training had not trained him to fight against the X-wing's maneuver. Colclazure told Thrawn that similar scenarios to Skywalker's escape had been part of Pieterson's training. The Chiss then explained the difference between an error and a mistake—that an error did not become a mistake until someone refused to correct it—to Colclazure as he ordered his [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]] to [[Death/Legends|behead]] Pieterson. Once the tractor beam operator was dead, the Grand Admiral told Pieterson's commander that his error had been corrected, instructing him to find a replacement for the dead conscript. Pieterson's death was witnessed by another tractor beam operator, [[Rejlii Mithel]], who expected a similar fate after also allowing Skywalker to escape during a subsequent encounter. Unlike, Pieterson, however, Mithel had impressed Thrawn by demonstrating innovative thinking during the incident and was promoted to lieutenant.''[[The Last Command]]'' ==Personality and traits== The tractor beam operator was willing to shift the blame onto his teacher and commander when he committed an error. Pieterson had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and fair skin. ==Equipment== Pieterson wore an [[Imperial Navy Trooper/Legends|Imperial Navy trooper's]] black uniform and a black protective [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]]. ==Behind the scenes== Cris Pieterson first appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[1991]] novel ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', the first installment of the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''Thrawn Trilogy'']]. Pieterson was later pictured in the [[Heir to the Empire 3|third]] issue of the novel's [[Dark Horse Comics]] [[Star Wars: Heir to the Empire (comic series)|comic-book adaptation]], written by [[Mike Baron]], pencilled by [[Olivier Vatine]] and [[Fred Blanchard]], and colored by [[Isabelle Rabarot]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/94-144/Star-Wars-Heir-to-the-Empire-3-of-6|text=STAR WARS: HEIR TO THE EMPIRE #3 (OF 6)}} Pieterson was a [[Tuckerization]] of a fan, Chris Peterson, who won a charity auction event that allowed him to be in Zahn's next book. Fellow winner Brian Colclazure, who's name was used for Ensign Colclazure, won the decision on whether Peterson's character would die.''[[Heir to the Empire: The 20th Anniversary Edition]]'' ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Cris Pieterson}} *''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' {{Ab|Heir to the Empire (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Heir to the Empire 3|''Heir to the Empire'' 3]] {{1stp}} *''[[The Last Command]]'' {{Ab|The Last Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Imo}} ==Sources== *{{GamerCite|5|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Chiss}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Cris Pieterson }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Pieterson, Cris}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Thrawn's fleet personnel]]
